name: Iowa Chop with Apricot Pecan Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried apricots in brandy and water for 30 minutes. Drain, reserving liquid, and chop apricots finely.
2. In a large bowl, combine chopped apricots with reserved liquid and onion mixture; set aside to cool slightly.
3. Add remaining stuffing ingredients (except bread crumbs) to the bowl, mixing well to incorporate eggs.
4. Stir in bread crumbs and refrigerate until ready to use.
5. Prepare pork chops by making a pocket horizontally toward the bone halfway up the thickness of each chop.
6. Stuff each chop with about 1/3 cup of the prepared stuffing.
7. Sear both sides of each chop in a skillet with vegetable oil.
8. Arrange chops in a baking pan, cover, and bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes, or until pork reaches 160°F internally.
9. Serve remaining stuffing as a side dish by placing it in a greased baking dish and baking uncovered until hot.
OPTIONAL FLAMBE:
1. Heat brandy in a small saucepan until hot but not boiling.
2. Pour hot brandy over the meat, then ignite with a lighted match. The flames will subside after a minute or so.
